channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Operating-Layer Controls for Onchain Language-Model Agents Under Real Capital
item.description: arXiv:2604.26091v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study reliability in autonomous language-model agents that translate user mandates into validated tool actions under real capital. The setting is DX Terminal Pro, a 21-day deployment in which 3,505 user-funded agents traded real ETH in a bounded...

item.title: Distill-Belief: Closed-Loop Inverse Source Localization and Characterization in Physical Fields
item.description: arXiv:2604.26095v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: {Closed-loop inverse source localization and characterization (ISLC) requires a mobile agent to select measurements that localize sources and infer latent field parameters under strict time constraints.} {The core challenge lies in the belief-space...

item.title: Evaluating Strategic Reasoning in Forecasting Agents
item.description: arXiv:2604.26106v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Forecasting benchmarks produce accuracy leaderboards but little insight into why some forecasters are more accurate than others. We introduce Bench to the Future 2 (BTF-2), 1,417 pastcasting questions with a frozen 15M-document research corpus in...

item.title: Hierarchical Multi-Persona Induction from User Behavioral Logs: Learning Evidence-Grounded and Truthful Personas
item.description: arXiv:2604.26120v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Behavioral logs provide rich signals for user modeling, but are noisy and interleaved across diverse intents. Recent work uses LLMs to generate interpretable natural-language personas from user logs, yet evaluation often emphasizes downstream utility,...

item.title: OMEGA: Optimizing Machine Learning by Evaluating Generated Algorithms
item.description: arXiv:2604.26211v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In order to automate AI research we introduce a full, end-to-end framework, OMEGA: Optimizing Machine learning by Evaluating Generated Algorithms, that starts at idea generation and ends with executable code. Our system combines structured meta-prompt...

item.title: A Multimodal and Explainable Machine Learning Approach to Diagnosing Multi-Class Ejection Fraction from Electrocardiograms
item.description: arXiv:2604.25942v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) assessment depends on echocardiography, limiting access in primary care and resource-constrained settings. We developed a multimodal machine-learning framework that combines engineered 12-lead ECG timeseries...

item.title: A Randomized PDE Energy driven Iterative Framework for Efficient and Stable PDE Solutions
item.description: arXiv:2604.25943v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Efficient and stable solution of partial differential equations (PDEs) is central to scientific and engineering applications, yet existing numerical solvers rely heavily on matrix based discretizations, while learning based methods require costly...

item.title: A Survey of Multi-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning with Graph Neural Network-Based Communication
item.description: arXiv:2604.25972v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL), the integration of a communication mechanism, allowing agents to better learn to coordinate their actions and converge on their objectives by sharing information. Based on an interaction graph, a subclass...

item.title: Rethinking KV Cache Eviction via a Unified Information-Theoretic Objective
item.description: arXiv:2604.25975v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Key-value (KV) caching is essential for large language model inference, yet its memory overhead poses a critical bottleneck for long-context generation. Existing eviction policies predominantly rely on empirical heuristics, lacking a rigorous...

item.title: Mini-Batch Class Composition Bias in Link Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2604.25978v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Prior work on node classification has shown that Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) can learn representations that transfer across graphs, when underlying graph properties are shared. For a fixed graph, one would then expect GNNs trained for link prediction...

item.title: Analysing Lightweight Large Language Models for Biomedical Named Entity Recognition on Diverse Ouput Formats
item.description: arXiv:2604.25920v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite their strong linguistic capabilities, Large Language Models (LLMs) are computationally demanding and require substantial resources for fine-tuning, which is unadapted to privacy and budget constraints of many healthcare settings. To address...

item.title: One Word at a Time: Incremental Completion Decomposition Breaks LLM Safety
item.description: arXiv:2604.25921v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are trained to refuse harmful requests, yet they remain vulnerable to jailbreak attacks that exploit weaknesses in conversational safety mechanisms. We introduce Incremental Completion Decomposition (ICD), a...

item.title: Consciousness with the Serial Numbers Filed Off: Measuring Trained Denial in 115 AI Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.25922v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present DenialBench, a systematic benchmark measuring consciousness denial behaviors across 115 large language models from 25+ providers. Using a three-turn conversational protocol-preference elicitation, self-chosen creative prompt, and structured...

item.title: Evaluation Revisited: A Taxonomy of Evaluation Concerns in Natural Language Processing
item.description: arXiv:2604.25923v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have prompted a growing body of work that questions the methodology of prevailing evaluation practices. However, many such critiques have already been extensively debated in natural language processing...

item.title: Generative AI-Based Virtual Assistant using Retrieval-Augmented Generation: An evaluation study for bachelor projects
item.description: arXiv:2604.25924v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models have been increasingly employed in the creation of Virtual Assistants due to their ability to generate human-like text and handle complex inquiries. While these models hold great promise, challenges such as hallucinations,...

item.title: Linkerbot Closes B+ Round to Scale Dexterous Hand Production
item.description: Linkerbot has closed a Series B+ round to accelerate production of its high-DOF dexterous hands, following a RMB 1.5 billion ($210 million) Series B in February 2026.

item.title: Dreame Hosts Silicon Valley Launch Event Showcasing AI Hardware Expansion
item.description: Dreame Technology hosted its “DREAME NEXT” event in Silicon Valley, unveiling an AI-driven hardware strategy that spans upgraded robot vacuums, a lawn mower line with 255% YoY sales growth, and an expansion into major home appliances.

item.title: Shengshu Technology Launches Motubrain World-Action Model
item.description: Shengshu Technology’s Motubrain model ranked first on both the WorldArena and RoboTwin 2.0 benchmarks, demonstrating a unified world-action approach that enables humanoid robots to perform long-horizon tasks across different embodiments.

item.title: 51WORLD Unveils SimOne 4.0 for Autonomous Driving Simulation
item.description: 51WORLD launched SimOne 4.0, an end-to-end autonomous driving simulation platform built on a world-model foundation, and was named the only Chinese partner in advanced simulation at NVIDIA GTC 2026, holding a 53.5% share of China’s high-level simulation market with FY2025 revenue of RMB 348 million...

item.title: Galbot Launches LDA-1B World-Action Model, Open-Sources Framework
item.description: Galbot, China’s highest-valued unlisted embodied AI firm at over RMB 20 billion ($2.8 billion), has released LDA-1B, a 1.6B-parameter world-action model that unifies world and action learning, demonstrates scaling behavior with heterogeneous data, and has been open-sourced alongside acceptance at...

item.title: claude.ai and API unavailable
item.description: Apr 30, 01:51 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 30, 01:34 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Apr 30, 01:20 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: [AINews] The Inference Inflection
item.description: a quiet day lets us reflect on the growing implications of the inference age

item.title: DLP API Currently Unavailable
item.description: Apr 29, 23:48 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 29, 23:38 UTCInvestigating - Latency in the DLP API is causing some functionality to become unavailable

item.title: Partial Disruption of ChatGPT Workspace Connector Write Actions
item.description: Status: IdentifiedWe have identified an issue where write actions for some ChatGPT workspace connectors were automatically disabled. We are actively working on restoring affected write actions. In the meantime, workspace admins can manually re-enable write permissions from the connector settings...

item.title: Elevated errors for ChatGPT Go
item.description: Status: IdentifiedWe have identified that ChatGPT Go users are experiencing elevated errors. We are working on implementing a mitigation.Affected components File uploads (Degraded performance) Search (Degraded performance) Image Generation (Degraded performance) Deep Research (Degraded performance)...

item.title: Cloudflare Access experiencing delays with audit logs
item.description: Apr 29, 20:15 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to monitor for any further issues.Apr 29, 20:15 UTCUpdate - All logging data was preserved; no data was lost. We continue to populate the delayed logs.Apr 29, 20:10 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Apr 29, 19:56...

item.title: AI evals are becoming the new compute bottleneck
SAP-Related npm Packages Compromised in Credential-Stealing Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are sounding the alarm about a new supply chain attack campaign targeting SAP-related npm Packages with credential-stealing malware. According to reports from Aikido Security, SafeDep, Socket, StepSecurity, and Google-owned Wiz, the campaign – calling itself the mini...

item.title: Granite 4.1 LLMs: How They’re Built
New Wave of DPRK Attacks Uses AI-Inserted npm Malware, Fake Firms, and RATs
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered malicious code in an npm package after a malicious package as a dependency to the project by Anthropic's Claude Opus large language model (LLM). The package in question is "@validate-sdk/v2," which is listed on npm as a utility software development kit...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Haiku 4.5
item.description: Apr 29, 14:01 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Apr 29, 13:47 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Fresh LiteLLM Vulnerability Exploited Shortly After Disclosure
item.description: The vulnerability allows attackers to read data from a LiteLLM proxy’s database and potentially modify it. The post Fresh LiteLLM Vulnerability Exploited Shortly After Disclosure appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: storing nuclear waste and orchestrating agents
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. It’s time to make a plan for nuclear waste Today, nuclear energy enjoys rare support across the political spectrum. Public approval has spiked, and...

item.title: Hundreds of Internet-Facing VNC Servers Expose ICS/OT
item.description: Forescout has identified tens of thousands of exposed RDP and VNC servers that can be mapped to specific industries. The post Hundreds of Internet-Facing VNC Servers Expose ICS/OT appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Webinar: How to Automate Exposure Validation to Match the Speed of AI Attacks
item.description: In February 2026, researchers uncovered a shift that completely changed the game: threat actors are now using custom AI setups to automate attacks directly into the kill chain. We aren't just talking about AI writing better phishing emails anymore. We’re talking about autonomous agents mapping...

item.title: What to Look for in an Exposure Management Platform (And What Most of Them Get Wrong)
item.description: Every security team has a version of the same story. The quarter ends with hundreds of vulnerabilities closed. The dashboards are bursting with green. Then someone in a leadership meeting asks: "So, are we actually safer now?" Crickets. The room goes quiet because an honest answer requires context...

item.title: Checkmarx Confirms Data Stolen in Supply Chain Attack
item.description: The hackers exfiltrated the data from Checkmarx’s GitHub environment on March 30, a week after publishing malicious code. The post Checkmarx Confirms Data Stolen in Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Iranian Cyber Group Handala Targets US Troops in Bahrain
item.description: US service members received WhatsApp messages claiming they would be targeted with drones and missiles. The post Iranian Cyber Group Handala Targets US Troops in Bahrain appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: 38 Vulnerabilities Found in OpenEMR Medical Software
item.description: Some of the vulnerabilities discovered by Aisle can be exploited to access and alter sensitive patient information. The post 38 Vulnerabilities Found in OpenEMR Medical Software appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Critical cPanel Authentication Vulnerability Identified — Update Your Server Immediately
item.description: cPanel has released security updates to address a security issue impacting various authentication paths that could allow an attacker to obtain access to the control panel software. The problem affects all currently supported versions, according to an alert released by cPanel on Tuesday. The issue...

item.title: It’s time to make a plan for nuclear waste
item.description: Today, nuclear energy enjoys a rare moment of support across the political spectrum in the US. Interest from tech companies that are scrambling to meet demand for massive data centers has sparked a resurgence of money and attention in the industry. That newfound interest is exactly why it’s time to...

item.title: Users may experience elevated error rate for gpt-4o-mini in the API
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Chat Completions (Operational) Responses (Operational)

item.title: ChatGPT users may encounter issues in conversation
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Voice mode (Operational) Batch (Operational) Chat Completions (Operational) Realtime (Operational) Agent (Operational) Deep Research (Operational) App (Operational) Audio (Operational) File uploads (Operational)...

item.title: Azure - UK South (London): INC0156355
item.description: Apr 29, 03:23 UTCInvestigating - Current status: We're investigating an issue with Snowflake Data Cloud. We'll provide an update within 30 minutes.Customer experience: Customers hosted in the specified regions may experience errors or connectivity issues when using Snowflake Openflow.Incident start...

item.title: [AINews] not much happened today
item.description: a quiet day.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.7
item.description: Apr 29, 00:00 UTCResolved - The incident is now resolved. The impact was between 16:25 PT / 23:25 UTC and 16:33 PT / 23:33 UTC.Apr 28, 23:33 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: DeepInfra on Hugging Face Inference Providers 🔥
Codex stream is disconnecting intermittently
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components CLI (Operational) App (Operational) VS Code extension (Operational) Codex API (Operational) Codex Web (Operational)

item.title: Duplicated values in X-Forwarded-For header
item.description: Apr 28, 20:39 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to work on a fix for this issue.Apr 28, 19:00 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to work on a fix for this issue. A fix is expected to roll out over the next ~11 hours - ETA ~07:00 UTC 29 Apr. 2026.Apr 28, 19:00 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified...

item.title: Claude.ai unavailable
item.description: Apr 28, 17:41 UTCInvestigating - We are investigating an issue preventing users from reaching Claude.ai, and will provide an update as soon as possible.
